questions.question: 1 (a) • Pour 75 cm 3 of hot water into beaker A. • Record, in the first row of Table 1.1, the temperature θ of the water in beaker A and immediately start the stopclock. • Record, in the table, the temperature θ of the water at times t = 30 s, 60 s, 90 s, 120 s, 150 s and 180 s. [1] (b) (i) Re

parts.text: 1 (a) • Pour 75 cm 3 of hot water into beaker A. • Record, in the first row of Table 1.1, the temperature θ of the water in beaker A and immediately start the stopclock. • Record, in the table, the temperature θ of the water at times t = 30 s, 60 s, 90 s, 120 s, 150 s and 180 s. [1] (b) (i) Re
parts.answer: θ for beaker A decreasing θ for beaker B decreasing, faster than for A and both to at least 1 ° C 1 1 units all correct (symbols or words) t values all present (30, 60, 90, 120, 150 and 180)

any appropriate precaution: e.g. avoidance of parallax(only if explained) stir before reading keep thermometer at same level wait until reading stops rising at the start conclusion matching results and correct mention of comparative temperature change ‘in same time’ 1 1 any suitable improvement to apparatus relating to comparison: e.g. insulate sides, use plastic beaker, stand on mat matching explanation: e.g. thermal energy only escapes from surface, surface area only variable changed, less transfer of thermal energy by sides appropriate effect on values of θ : e.g. all higher Page 4 Mark Scheme Syllabus Paper 0625 53

any appropriate factor: e.g. volume of water, initial temperature of water, similar ratio of surface areas, type / material of beaker, room temperature / appropriate environmental factor Total / values, all increasing all < 1.00 A and to 2dp at least 1 1
correct calculations of R axes labelled with quantity and unit appropriate scales (plots occupying at least ½ grid) plots all correct to ½ small square well-judged line and thin line, precise plots
simple statement matching line (e.g. increases with p.d.) qualified (e.g. changes less rapidly for greater p.d. values)

correct symbol for variable resistor (rectangle with strike-through arrow only) in correct series circuit Total Page 5 Mark Scheme Syllabus Paper 0625 53 sensible values for h O (1.0 to 2.5 (cm)) and h I ( h O ±0.5 (cm)) M calculation correct no unit for M 1 1 f in range 14 to 16 (cm) to 2/3 sig figs and with unit of cm any appropriate difficulty: e.g. hand / ruler in way of image matching improvement: e.g. use translucent screen and view from behind, fix ruler / grid to screen 3(b((i) v = 24 ± 2 (cm) f within 10% of f statement matching results with justification matching statement (‘with in limits of experimental accuracy’/owtte)

any suitable precaution: e.g. dark room, (centre of) lens and object same height (above bench), lens / object / screen perpendicular, ruler fixed / placed on bench, mark centre of lens on holder Total Page 6 Mark Scheme Syllabus Paper 0625 53 4 apparatus – workable arrangement how applied force is measured suitable table for results / plot a bar graph how to conclude which is strongest one suitable control variable: e.g. same width of sample same thickness / weight / length of paper all samples fixed in same way any 2 from: 2nd control variable, force applied smoothly / no jerking ensure no tears before applying force repeat for each type of sample / repeat with samples of different widths soft mat under weights (to cushion fall) / clamp stand to bench add weight of lower block to value of load any other suitable precaution Total
